# WI-QUIC-CRT-0036 - QUIC CRT internal early-data prerequisite capture work item

## Summary

Capture the minimum dormant early-data prerequisite material behind the detached resumption carrier while keeping early data explicitly closed and the accepted resumption boundary unchanged.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0139

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0034

## Out of Scope

- 0-RTT packet send/receive support
- anti-replay enforcement
- key update
- public API widening
- IsSupported widening
- broader resumption claims
- broad post-handshake TLS support
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to the new early-data prerequisite carrier

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for positive capture, dormant client setup storage, missing early-data-signal absence, closed early-data admission, the public no-0-RTT guard, and the accepted-resumption regression; then run the relevant CRT/API/INT guard set, the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep, and SpecTrace render/validation checks.

## Completion Notes

The managed client/runtime path now retains the ticket's `max_early_data_size` hint together with the cloned peer transport-parameter snapshot as dormant internal state, but early data stays explicitly closed and no public resumption promise widens.
